opportunities.title: Deathbringer RPG: What Are the Actual Rules? (Not Just the Vibe)
opportunities.why_now: Deathbringer RPG was just revealed on EN World's Podcast #406 livestream, giving us the first look at its system before hype outpaces facts.
opportunities.angle: Rules-first breakdown post: pull every confirmed mechanic, tag what's still unknown, and challenge readers to spot the gaps between marketing copy and actual rules text.

opportunities.title: Arcana Unleashed's New Backgrounds: What They Actually Do at the Table
opportunities.why_now: Arcana Unleashed backgrounds were just teased in the same podcast, and background subsystems are exactly the kind of crunch this channel dissects.
opportunities.angle: Mechanics-first thread: list each background's stated benefit, flag any that duplicate existing feats/skills, and rate them by rules impact vs. flavor text.

opportunities.title: The $500 Deadfall Box Set: Pricing the Rules, Not the Minis
opportunities.why_now: A $500 box set announcement is a rare price point that demands a hard look at what rules content actually justifies the cost.
opportunities.angle: Cost-per-rule analysis: break down what's actually new mechanically vs. what's just premium components, with a verdict on value for GMs who care about crunch.

opportunities.title: Sly Flourish's 'Adventure Types' Aren't New — Here's Where They're Already Written
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish just published a fresh taxonomy of adventure types from Rise of the Lazy Gamemaster, prime territory for a rules-literalist cross-check.
opportunities.angle: Rebuttal/companion post: match each named adventure type to existing published rules structures (D&D DMG, other systems) to show the framework isn't new, just newly labeled.

opportunities.title: This Week's Free & Discounted TTRPGs: Ranked by Rules Density
opportunities.why_now: EN World's July 19 freebies/sales/charity bundle roundup just dropped, and most 'best of' lists ignore actual mechanical depth.
opportunities.angle: Curated ranking: sort the bundle's contents by crunch level (rules-light to rules-heavy) so readers can pick based on system complexity, not just price.
